reasoning: The March 2021 LHCb 3.1 sigma anomaly that motivated the question was resolved in December 2022, when LHCb published updated results showing perfect agreement with Standard Model lepton universality. Furthermore, recent precision measurements from ATLAS in 2024 also confirm lepton universality. There are no mentions of any new 5 sigma violations. Given that the knowledge cutoff is late October 2025 and only two months remain before the resolution date, it is virtually impossible for a new anomaly to be discovered, analyzed, subjected to peer review, and published by the end of 2025. Therefore, the probability is at the minimal threshold.
batch_queries: Will lepton universality be falsified before 2026?
